posts.raw_content: “Cruel and unusual” punishment. Notice it isn’t “cruel OR unusual”. Because the point of the amendment was the “unusual” part. Nobody in 1790s America had a problem with corporal punishment when duly convicted. They had a problem with “unusual” punishment, meaning outside the process of the law (due process). They saw British courts torture people before trial, to get false confessions. That’s what the 8th amendment is meant to stop. It really isn’t supposed to affect the punishment AFTER conviction. The point was to bar the government from extralegally torturing you for a false confession. But now it’s the “oh no you can’t deny murderers and rapists their Xbox in prison” type of shit. Which is not the point of the amendment and perverts it into some pro criminal bullshit. The founders hated criminals, the people who wrote the 13th amendment after an entire civil war, still kept the part about involuntary labor when duly convicted. Because they didn’t have a problem with punishing convicts. My rant.
